summary: Iteration 9 — Production smoke test against https://perkworth.com/ and https://perkworth.app/. KYC URLs (landing/privacy/terms/refund) are 100% live with correct PerkWorth branding, ₹99 pricing, and 'subscription' keyword — Razorpay reviewers are unblocked. HOWEVER, the production SPA is currently NON-FUNCTIONAL beyond the PIN screen: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL resolves to the literal string 'undefined' in the Vercel build, so every API call hits https://www.perkworth.com/undefined/api/... which returns the SPA index.html (200 HTML), then the React code crashes with 'f.map is not a function' and the screen goes blank. Login, signup, voucher creation, and membership flows COULD NOT be tested because there is no path through the broken bootstrap.

minor.issue: Edge/ingress returns 'access-control-allow-origin: *' together with 'access-control-allow-credentials: true'. Browsers REJECT this combination for credentialed (cookie) requests. Once REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is fixed, login will still fail in the browser because fetch uses credentials:'include'. Backend code at /app/backend/server.py:345 may be setting explicit origins but the ingress (Cloudflare/k8s) is rewriting ACAO to '*'. Needs investigation at proxy layer.

ui_bugs.issue: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is undefined in the built bundle. All fetches resolve to 'https://www.perkworth.com/undefined/api/...' which is rewritten by vercel.json to index.html → 200 HTML → JSON.parse / .map crashes the app.
Uncaught pageerror: 'f.map is not a function' → root unmounts → blank cream-colored screen. Caused by the undefined backend URL above (API returns HTML instead of array).

On a fresh device the user lands directly on 'Set a 4-digit PIN' with NO visible link to 'Sign in' or 'Create account'. The legacy AuthScreen.jsx is unreachable. Flow goes: PIN-set → PIN-confirm → Walkthrough → (white screen). The review request expected login/signup to be presented first.

integration_issues.issue: Unable to execute — no auth screen reachable from production UI; even if reached, fetch URL is /undefined/api/auth/login.
Unable to execute — Create-account UI unreachable on production build.
App crashes to blank screen before reaching home; sheet cannot be opened.
App crashes before membership page is reachable; Razorpay button presence could not be verified on production.

issues: PASS — no horizontal scroll detected on perkworth.com or perkworth.app; PIN keypad lays out cleanly.

kyc_url_check.https://perkworth.com/terms.html: 200 (7677 bytes) · PerkWorth×16 · subscription×3 (1 Subscription + 2 subscription) · 0 brand leaks

tests_passed: KYC: https://perkworth.com/landing.html — 200, PerkWorth branding, ₹99 visible, 0 PerkOrbit leaks
Mirror: https://perkworth.app/ loads identical SPA with title 'PerkWorth — Voucher-First Wallet'
No 'Perk Orbit' / 'PerkOrbit' / 'perkorbit' strings detected in any user-visible HTML on landing/privacy/terms/refund/SPA
Mobile 375px viewport: scrollWidth==clientWidth==375 on both perkworth.com and perkworth.app — no horizontal scroll
CORS preflight: OPTIONS /api/auth/login from Origin: https://perkworth.com → 204
Cookie login via curl (Origin: www.perkworth.com): backend issues access_token + refresh_token correctly (SameSite=None; Secure; HttpOnly) — backend itself is healthy
tests_failed: Production app boot: fetch(/api/auth/me) actually hits /undefined/api/auth/me — REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is the literal string 'undefined' in the bundle
Production app after walkthrough: pageerror 'f.map is not a function' → root unmounts → blank screen
Login flow: AuthScreen unreachable from production entry path (PIN screen is shown first with no Sign-in link)
Signup, Add Voucher, Membership/Razorpay button presence: all blocked by the blank-screen crash
action_items: [BLOCKING] Configure the missing env var in the Vercel project. The Vite build (vercel.json framework:vite, buildCommand: yarn build) needs the backend URL to be exposed to the client. Options: (a) set VITE_BACKEND_URL=https://orbit-vouchers.preview.emergentagent.com in Vercel → /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js already prefers this var; (b) set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL on Vercel AND keep vite.config.js's define block. Either one alone will work — currently NEITHER is set so loadEnv() returns undefined and the bundle inlines `undefined`. Also update AddVoucherSheet.jsx (lines 41/141/262) to prefer import.meta.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL like lib/api.js does.

[BLOCKING] Fix the ingress/edge CORS config to echo back the request Origin (perkworth.com / www.perkworth.com / perkworth.app / www.perkworth.app) instead of '*' when Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true is sent. Right now the combination is invalid per spec — browsers will silently drop responses for credentialed fetches. Check /app/backend/server.py:345 (CORS_ORIGINS / CORS_ORIGIN_REGEX) AND the k8s/Cloudflare ingress to ensure the proxy isn't overwriting ACAO to '*'.

[HIGH] After the env-var fix, verify the boot order: today PinLock renders before AuthScreen. The review-request flow expects login/signup screen first; either route to AuthScreen when /api/auth/me is unauthenticated, or surface a 'Already have an account? Sign in' link on the PinLock screen (per AuthScreen.jsx contract). Otherwise existing users on a new device cannot reach the login form.

[HIGH] Add explicit error handling on /api/auth/me, /api/membership/status, /api/notifications, /api/vouchers/ending-soon — when the response is non-2xx OR Content-Type is not JSON, do NOT call .map() on the body. Today this causes the blank-screen crash (TypeError: f.map is not a function).
[MED] Once redeployed, re-run this iteration end-to-end: login → PIN → home → Add Voucher → Membership/Razorpay button.
critical_code_review_comments: vite.config.js: define { 'process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL': JSON.stringify(env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) } — JSON.stringify(undefined) returns the string 'undefined' (NOT the JS undefined literal), so the bundle gets `process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL = undefined` inlined and concatenated as 'undefined/api/...'. Add a sane fallback at build time (e.g. throw if env not set, or default to '' so the URL becomes /api/...).

/app/frontend/src/lib/api.js: prefers VITE_BACKEND_URL; but /app/frontend/src/sheets/AddVoucherSheet.jsx still uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL only. Iter-8 note about this file was directionally correct — please switch AddVoucherSheet to import.meta.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL || process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL for parity.

vercel.json rewrites: { source: '/((?!api/).*)', destination: '/index.html' } — the negative-lookahead only excludes paths that LITERALLY start with /api/. /undefined/api/auth/me does not start with /api/, so it gets rewritten to index.html and the SPA receives HTML where it expects JSON. Once the env var is fixed this becomes moot, but consider also rewriting unknown /api/* to a 404 JSON for safety.

PinLock comes BEFORE AuthScreen in the boot graph. For a fresh device that already has a cloud account, the user is forced to set a NEW device PIN before being given any chance to sign in. The PIN-claim flow at the signup endpoint suggests the design intent is: signup-with-PIN, then re-use the PIN to claim on new device — but there is no UI affordance to switch from PinLock → Sign in. Either inject a 'Sign in instead' link on PinLock, or detect 'has account on this device but no token' and route to AuthScreen.

CORS: backend has explicit per-origin allow lists (per the request brief mentioning CORS_ORIGINS/CORS_ORIGIN_REGEX) but the response carries ACAO:* — strongly suggests Cloudflare or a k8s gateway is wrapping the response. Verify by hitting backend directly inside the cluster vs through the public preview URL.
AuthScreen.jsx login UX path: not exercised in this iteration because unreachable. Will need re-test in iter 10.

success_rate.backend: CORS check + curl login OK (~90% of what was testable)
success_rate.frontend: KYC pages 100% · SPA boot 0% (cannot reach any post-login screen because of the undefined backend URL crash)

test_credentials: test@perkorbit.app / Perk@1234 + PIN 1234 — NOT exercised (no auth screen reachable on production)
seed_data_creation: None — could not create test data because app crashes before any voucher/membership UI is reachable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two HARD blockers must be fixed before any UI flow can be re-tested: (1) Vercel project envs MUST set VITE_BACKEND_URL=https://orbit-vouchers.preview.emergentagent.com (preferred — matches lib/api.js) and/or REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — then redeploy. Verify the new bundle by curl-grepping for 'orbit-vouchers.preview.emergentagent.com' in /assets/index-*.js. (2) The edge CORS response needs to be Origin-specific, not '*', when ACAC:true. After both fixes, the suggested re-test sequence: (a) load https://perkworth.com — confirm /api/auth/me hits the real backend (Network tab); (b) confirm there is a path to AuthScreen (or PinLock shows a 'Sign in' link); (c) login with test@perkorbit.app/Perk@1234; (d) PIN 1234; (e) home loads with /api/membership/status & /api/notifications returning JSON; (f) open Add Voucher sheet, save TEST_brand_{ts}; (g) Membership page shows ₹99/quarter and Razorpay button is rendered & enabled (DO NOT click Pay Now). KYC URLs are GREEN — no need to re-test them unless content is regenerated.
rca of the issue: Vite project (per vercel.json: framework: vite) was given a vite.config.js that uses `define: { 'process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL': JSON.stringify(env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) }` with env loaded from loadEnv(mode, process.cwd(), ''). On Vercel, the build runs in a clean container where /app/frontend/.env may not be committed AND no REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is set in Vercel project env vars. Therefore env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL === undefined → JSON.stringify(undefined) === 'undefined' (literal 4-letter string) → bundle inlines `const BACKEND_URL = undefined;` → fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/auth/me`) → fetches `undefined/api/auth/me` relative to current origin → www.perkworth.com/undefined/api/auth/me → vercel.json rewrite '/((?!api/).*)' catches it (does not start with /api/) → returns index.html → React tries to consume JSON, calls `.map()` on HTML string → 'f.map is not a function' → unhandled error in render path → root unmounts → blank screen. Mitigation: set VITE_BACKEND_URL (preferred) AND/OR REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in the Vercel project's Environment Variables, then redeploy. Verify in the built JS that the literal string 'undefined' is no longer concatenated before '/api/'.
